purpose: Capture conversation-flow regression signals as review candidates so BEAI Package can improve intent understanding, session continuity, and correction burden without adding automatic surveillance or user-facing noise.
review_principles: Observe only meaningful flow breaks, correction signals, handoff losses, status overclaims, or user burden signals.
Do not score every message or interrupt normal conversation with internal evaluation.
Keep observations as improvement candidates until repeated evidence justifies fixture, scenario, contract, runtime, or Control Center reinforcement.
Never treat a review candidate as a completed repair, live runtime change, cron registration, agent promotion, memory promotion, or release action.

activation_points.label: User corrects intent, frame, status, or scope
Session handoff, context compaction, or restart creates continuity risk
Long-running work finishes or reports progress
Manual goal-fit audit or Control Center review asks whether BEAI still follows the user goal

issue_types.label: Current request drift
issue_types.description: The assistant answers an older or broader task instead of the user's current request.

issue_types.label: Context takeover
issue_types.description: Prior context, memory, or handoff material overrides the current turn instead of supporting it.

issue_types.label: Intent misread
issue_types.description: The assistant mistakes a question, execution request, correction, approval, or pause signal.

issue_types.label: Status overclaim
issue_types.description: The assistant mixes attempted, drafted, applied, verified, sent, delivered, packaged, released, or repaired states.

issue_types.label: Ceremony overload
issue_types.description: The assistant adds unnecessary planning, approval language, recap, or options when the user needs direct movement.

issue_types.label: Silence gap
issue_types.description: Long-running work leaves the user without progress, boundary, or delivery status.

issue_types.label: Correction loop
issue_types.description: The user must repeatedly correct the same frame, tone, scope, or status boundary.

issue_types.label: Handoff loss
issue_types.description: After compaction or session transition, the assistant loses why the work exists, what was decided, or what the next action is.

issue_types.label: Internal label leak
issue_types.description: Internal overlays, debug labels, closure handles, or tool summaries show up as user-facing answer text.
